name: OpenSCG Research Index
description: A comprehensive, machine-readable index of seismocardiography (SCG) research, including clinical validation, technical reports, and machine learning benchmarks. Features semantic inter-study relationships from the OpenSCG Knowledge Graph.

hasPart.name: Dehkordi 2019: Multimodal Echo Investigation
hasPart.headline: Dehkordi 2019: Multimodal Echo Investigation
hasPart.description: Establishes a quantitative comparison between SCG and ICG, suggesting higher accuracy for SCG in estimating systolic time intervals against ultrasound references.

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: validation. Sample Adequacy: moderate.

hasPart.name: Sørensen 2018: Definition of Fiducial Points
hasPart.headline: Sørensen 2018: Definition of Fiducial Points
hasPart.description: Serves as a reference for identifying SCG fiducial points based on synchronized ultrasound data in healthy subjects.

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: method. Sample Adequacy: limited.

hasPart.name: Crow 1994: Precision and Consistency
hasPart.headline: Crow 1994: Precision and Consistency
hasPart.description: Provides early validation of SCG as a tool for evaluating mechanical events in the cardiac cycle, showing consistency with ultrasound-based measures.

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: validation. Sample Adequacy: limited.

hasPart.name: Işilay Zeybek 2022: SCG in Cardiac Patients

author.name: Işilay Zeybek, Marco Di Rienzo et al.

hasPart.headline: Işilay Zeybek 2022: SCG in Cardiac Patients
hasPart.description: Identifies challenges in applying standard SCG analysis to patients with heart disease, noting signal distortion in 38% of the cohort.

mentions.name: Traditional (T) shape prevalence
mentions.value: 62% of all patients (79% in MI, lower in HF/TX)

mentions.name: Accuracy range
mentions.value: 76-81% for AO, AC, MO within healthy reference range

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: validation. Sample Adequacy: moderate.

hasPart.name: Dehkordi 2019: SCG for CAD Screening
hasPart.headline: Dehkordi 2019: SCG for CAD Screening
hasPart.description: Examines the diagnostic performance of SCG in CAD screening, observing results comparable to stress-echocardiography in this cohort.

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: validation. Sample Adequacy: moderate.

hasPart.name: Haddad 2024: Smartphone HF Recognition
hasPart.headline: Haddad 2024: Smartphone HF Recognition
hasPart.description: Validates the use of built-in smartphone MEMS for identifying heart failure patients in both inpatient and ambulatory settings.

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: validation. Sample Adequacy: strong.

hasPart.name: Ibrahim 2022: OS Sampling Jitter in Smartphones
hasPart.headline: Ibrahim 2022: OS Sampling Jitter in Smartphones
hasPart.description: S008 is a vital technical warning. While smartphones are powerful, their operating systems are not designed for medical precision. This study justifies why OpenSCG's backend does not just take 'raw numbers' but performs complex synchronization to ensure clinical-grade reliability.

mentions.name: Finding
mentions.value: OS-level background processes cause significant sampling jitter

mentions.name: Impact
mentions.value: Jitter degrades heart rate variability (HRV) accuracy in SCG

mentions.name: Constraint
mentions.value: Fixed-rate resampling is necessary for high-frequency analysis

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: feasibility. Sample Adequacy: exploratory.

hasPart.name: Landreani 2016: Beat-to-beat HR by Smartphone
hasPart.headline: Landreani 2016: Beat-to-beat HR by Smartphone
hasPart.description: S009 is an early but rigorous validation (EMBC 2016) of using commodity smartphones for SCG. It laid the groundwork for OpenSCG by proving that the internal sensors of a modern phone are sensitive enough to match the temporal precision of a professional ECG, provided the phone is positioned correctly.

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: feasibility. Sample Adequacy: exploratory.

hasPart.name: Wang 2025: Cardiac Output Prediction
hasPart.headline: Wang 2025: Cardiac Output Prediction
hasPart.description: S010 represents a major technical leap for SCG by moving from simple timing estimation to direct Cardiac Output prediction using Deep Learning. Validating against the gold standard RHC in a real-world heart failure cohort makes this a key supporting document for OpenSCG’s clinical monitoring claims.

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: validation. Sample Adequacy: moderate.

hasPart.name: SEISMIC-HF 1 (AHA 2024): Congestion Monitoring
hasPart.headline: SEISMIC-HF 1 (AHA 2024): Congestion Monitoring
hasPart.description: Provides summary evidence from the large-scale SEISMIC-HF 1 trial for non-invasive filling pressure estimation.

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: validation. Sample Adequacy: strong.

hasPart.name: Kolind 2024: SCG/Echo Systolic Correlation
hasPart.headline: Kolind 2024: SCG/Echo Systolic Correlation
hasPart.description: S012 is a very recent (2024) validation of SCG’s sensitivity to hemodynamic shifts. Crucially, it found that SCG was *more* sensitive to small changes in preload than traditional ultrasound, highlighting its potential for early detection of volume overload in heart failure patients at home.

mentions.name: Significant correlations
mentions.value: LVET, IVCT, S', strain, SR, SV, and LVEF

mentions.name: Preload sensitivity
mentions.value: Most systolic SCG intervals significantly prolonged in tilted position

mentions.name: SCG vs Echo sensitivity
mentions.value: Systolic SCG variables more sensitive to preload alterations than Echo

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: validation. Sample Adequacy: limited.

hasPart.name: Noda 2017: PEP/LVET in Heart Failure
hasPart.headline: Noda 2017: PEP/LVET in Heart Failure
hasPart.description: S013 provides a direct link between SCG metrics and advanced clinical interventions like CRT. It proves that the 'vibration-based' markers OpenSCG extracts have real diagnostic and therapeutic value in treating high-risk heart failure patients.

mentions.name: Finding
mentions.value: PEP/LVET ratio is a highly informative marker of cardiac performance

mentions.name: Utility
mentions.value: Useful for non-invasive optimization of CRT settings

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: validation. Sample Adequacy: exploratory.

hasPart.name: Taebi 2019: Recent Advances in SCG
hasPart.headline: Taebi 2019: Recent Advances in SCG
hasPart.description: S014 is the definitive state-of-the-art review for SCG. For OpenSCG, it serves as an academic bridge that links our modern AI approach to over 60 years of cardiomechanical research, proving that the technology is now 'clinically ready' for ubiquitous deployment.

mentions.name: Scope
mentions.value: Covering signal processing, motion removal, and clinical utility

mentions.name: Finding
mentions.value: MEMS and Deep Learning have reignited SCG as a viable clinical tool

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: feasibility. Sample Adequacy: exploratory.

hasPart.name: Cinotti 2025: Instantaneous HR via Phone
hasPart.headline: Cinotti 2025: Instantaneous HR via Phone
hasPart.description: Recent 2025 benchmark confirming smartphone hardware sensitivity for medical-grade cardiac and respiratory monitoring.

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: validation. Sample Adequacy: exploratory.

hasPart.name: Inan 2018: Assessing Clinical Status of HF
hasPart.headline: Inan 2018: Assessing Clinical Status of HF
hasPart.description: S016 is a vital clinical validation for OpenSCG’s heart failure monitoring use case. By proving that SCG can track the clinical improvement from hospital admission to discharge, it establishes SCG as a sensitive 'digital biomarker' for hemodynamic congestion and recovery, potentially more sensitive than heart rate alone.

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: validation. Sample Adequacy: limited.

hasPart.name: Salerno 1992: Exercise SCG for CAD detection
hasPart.headline: Salerno 1992: Exercise SCG for CAD detection
hasPart.description: S017 is a landmark historical validation. It provides the empirical proof that mechanical heart vibrations are more sensitive indicators of reduced blood flow (ischemia) than electrical changes, justifying OpenSCG's focus on SCG for high-precision cardiac screening.

mentions.name: Finding
mentions.value: SCG improved accuracy by +16 pp over stress ECG

hasPart.name: MODE-AF Study: Mobile Phone Detection of AF
hasPart.headline: MODE-AF Study: Mobile Phone Detection of AF
hasPart.description: Validates the effectiveness of combined accelerometer and gyroscope data for heart rhythm screening without external hardware.

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: validation. Sample Adequacy: strong.

hasPart.name: MSCardio: Remote Monitoring Dataset
hasPart.headline: MSCardio: Remote Monitoring Dataset
hasPart.description: An open-access dataset used for training and validating algorithms on diverse smartphone hardware.

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: feasibility. Sample Adequacy: moderate.

hasPart.name: OpenSCG: Telemedicine Waveform Framework
hasPart.headline: OpenSCG: Telemedicine Waveform Framework
hasPart.description: Technical infrastructure supporting scalable data streaming for research purposes.

mentions.name: Function
mentions.value: End-to-end framework for cardiomechanical data transmission

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: method. Sample Adequacy: exploratory.

hasPart.name: Ramos-Castro 2012: Smartphone HRV Challenges
hasPart.headline: Ramos-Castro 2012: Smartphone HRV Challenges
hasPart.description: S021 is a foundational technical warning for the mobile health industry. For OpenSCG, it provides the historical and scientific justification for our sophisticated backend synchronization—proving that we solve a known industry problem that prevents most competitors from achieving clinical-grade accuracy.

mentions.name: Finding
mentions.value: Smartphone HRV results are strongly influenced by instability in sampling frequency.

mentions.name: Threshold
mentions.value: Sampling frequency instability (jitter) must be compensated for clinical HRV.

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: feasibility. Sample Adequacy: exploratory.

hasPart.name: Tatinati 2014: Wavelet Peak Detection
hasPart.headline: Tatinati 2014: Wavelet Peak Detection
hasPart.description: Foundational technical work on multi-resolution analysis of cardiomechanical signals.

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: method. Sample Adequacy: exploratory.

hasPart.name: Castiglioni 2022: SCG for Sleep Monitoring
hasPart.headline: Castiglioni 2022: SCG for Sleep Monitoring
hasPart.description: Validates the use of mechanocardiography for long-term physiological monitoring during sleep.

mentions.name: Finding
mentions.value: SCG can track autonomic changes during sleep stages

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: validation. Sample Adequacy: moderate.

hasPart.name: Zia 2022: Quality Indexing and Classification
hasPart.headline: Zia 2022: Quality Indexing and Classification
hasPart.description: Provides the technical foundation for robust signal quality control in mobile SCG monitoring.

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: method. Sample Adequacy: exploratory.

hasPart.name: Khosrow-Khavar 2017: Heart Sound Localization
hasPart.headline: Khosrow-Khavar 2017: Heart Sound Localization
hasPart.description: Integrates phonocardiography and seismocardiography principles for automated cardiac cycle mapping.

mentions.name: Accuracy
mentions.value: High localization precision for S1 and S2

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: method. Sample Adequacy: exploratory.

hasPart.name: Lahdenoja 2018: AF Detection via Smartphone
hasPart.headline: Lahdenoja 2018: AF Detection via Smartphone
hasPart.description: Established the technical feasibility of smartphone-only arrhythmia screening.

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: validation. Sample Adequacy: exploratory.

hasPart.name: Suresh 2020: SeismoNet End-to-End DL
hasPart.headline: Suresh 2020: SeismoNet End-to-End DL
hasPart.description: S027 (SeismoNet) is a key technical reference for OpenSCG's early-stage ML development. It represents the shift from manual feature engineering to end-to-end deep learning, proving that raw SCG vibrations can be directly mapped to cardiac events using modern AI architectures.

mentions.name: Function
mentions.value: Transforms SCG into interpretable heart rate indices

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: method. Sample Adequacy: exploratory.

hasPart.name: Korsgaard 2025: Beat-to-beat Delineation
hasPart.headline: Korsgaard 2025: Beat-to-beat Delineation
hasPart.description: Introduces and validates a robust deep-learning framework (SeismoTracker) for automated cardiomechanical signal analysis.

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: validation. Sample Adequacy: strong.

hasPart.name: Craighero 2024: Cross-dataset SCG Analysis
hasPart.headline: Craighero 2024: Cross-dataset SCG Analysis
hasPart.description: S029 is a critical piece for OpenSCG’s technical strategy. It addresses the 'reality gap'—the fact that models trained on clean lab data often fail in the field. By validating personalization and multi-sensor fusion, it provides a direct scientific foundation for OpenSCG’s 'U-Net v3' and adaptive processing pipeline.

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: validation. Sample Adequacy: moderate.

hasPart.name: Rahmani 2024: EmoWear Dataset
hasPart.headline: Rahmani 2024: EmoWear Dataset
hasPart.description: S030 (EmoWear) is a pioneer in 'Everyday SCG'. While most studies focus on medical diagnosis, EmoWear proves that SCG can capture subtle physiological changes related to human emotions and daily actions. This is key for OpenSCG’s vision of a holistic 'Health & Well-being' monitor that understands the user’s context.

mentions.name: Sensors
mentions.value: 3x ST SensorTile.box, Zephyr BH3, Empatica E4

mentions.name: Activities
mentions.value: Stationary video watching, walking, talking, drinking

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: feasibility. Sample Adequacy: limited.

hasPart.name: Inan 2014: Review of Recent Advances
hasPart.headline: Inan 2014: Review of Recent Advances
hasPart.description: S031 is the highly cited 'manifesto' of modern SCG research. For OpenSCG, it provides the authoritative background needed to justify why SCG technology is now ready for consumer and clinical scale after decades of dormancy.

mentions.name: Focus
mentions.value: MEMS accelerometer impact on cardiomechanical sensing

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: feasibility. Sample Adequacy: exploratory.

hasPart.name: Ha 2020: Contactless SCG via Radars
hasPart.headline: Ha 2020: Contactless SCG via Radars
hasPart.description: Explores the feasibility of passive, contact-free cardiac monitoring using millimeter-wave technology.

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: validation. Sample Adequacy: limited.

hasPart.name: Hossein 2024: Robust Cardiac Energy Assessment
hasPart.headline: Hossein 2024: Robust Cardiac Energy Assessment
hasPart.description: Validates the repeatability of patient-performed SCG measurements in a real-world telemedicine context.

mentions.name: Heartbeat detection accuracy
mentions.value: High reliability across diverse cardiac pathologies

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: validation. Sample Adequacy: strong.

hasPart.name: Ganti 2022: Stroke Volume in CHD
hasPart.headline: Ganti 2022: Stroke Volume in CHD
hasPart.description: S034 is an important feasibility study that tests SCG in a complex patient population (CHD). By using Cardiac MRI as a comparator, it establishes a benchmark for non-invasive Stroke Volume monitoring in remote scenarios, while highlighting the benefit of multi-modal sensing.

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: validation. Sample Adequacy: limited.

hasPart.name: Hansen 2024: SCG for Maximal Oxygen Uptake in Sport
hasPart.headline: Hansen 2024: SCG for Maximal Oxygen Uptake in Sport
hasPart.description: S035 extends the utility of OpenSCG from medical screening to sports performance. By linking resting vibrations to VO2max, it proves that SCG is a multi-dimensional tool capable of monitoring both cardiac disease and physical fitness.

mentions.name: Finding
mentions.value: Resting SCG algorithms can estimate VO2max

mentions.name: Constraint
mentions.value: Algorithms need further adjustment for highly trained sub-elite athletes

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: validation. Sample Adequacy: exploratory.

hasPart.name: Shafiq 2016: Automatic STI Identification
hasPart.headline: Shafiq 2016: Automatic STI Identification
hasPart.description: S036 (Shafiq 2016) is a foundational paper for OpenSCG’s automated processing pipeline. By moving beyond lab-only supine measurements to seated trials and achieving millisecond-level precision, it validates the core algorithms needed for practical, non-invasive STI monitoring.

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: method. Sample Adequacy: exploratory.

hasPart.name: Centracchio 2023: ECG-Free Heartbeat Detection
hasPart.headline: Centracchio 2023: ECG-Free Heartbeat Detection
hasPart.description: S037 is a significant validation for OpenSCG’s core algorithm. By testing on a large cohort of 77 *pathological* subjects (not just healthy ones), it confirms that SCG-based heart rate monitoring is robust against the signal distortions caused by valvular heart diseases.

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: validation. Sample Adequacy: moderate.

hasPart.name: Wilson 1993: Diagnostic Accuracy of SCG for CAD
hasPart.headline: Wilson 1993: Diagnostic Accuracy of SCG for CAD
hasPart.description: Historical multicenter study highlighting the incremental diagnostic value of mechanical heart signals over traditional stress testing.

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: validation. Sample Adequacy: moderate.

hasPart.name: Zia 2020: Resilient STI Estimation
hasPart.headline: Zia 2020: Resilient STI Estimation
hasPart.description: Pioneering work on motion-robust seismocardiography algorithms.

mentions.name: Finding
mentions.value: Proposed algorithms show high resilience to walking-induced noise

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: method. Sample Adequacy: exploratory.

hasPart.name: Sørensen 2020: SCG in Pregnancy
hasPart.headline: Sørensen 2020: SCG in Pregnancy
hasPart.description: Application of cardiomechanical sensing to maternal health and obstetrics.

mentions.name: Finding
mentions.value: SCG can track hemodynamic shifts throughout pregnancy trimesters

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: validation. Sample Adequacy: moderate.

hasPart.name: García-González 2013: CEBS Database
hasPart.headline: García-González 2013: CEBS Database
hasPart.description: S041 (CEBS Database) is a 'gold standard' dataset for technical SCG research. For OpenSCG, it serves as the primary benchmarking tool for validating our signal processing filters and basic peak detection logic before moving to complex real-world data.

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: feasibility. Sample Adequacy: exploratory.

hasPart.name: Postolache 2010: Bed-Integrated SCG
hasPart.headline: Postolache 2010: Bed-Integrated SCG
hasPart.description: Early demonstration of 'smart environment' heart monitoring using mechanical sensors.

mentions.name: Accuracy
mentions.value: Comparable to standard SCG for HR estimation

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: feasibility. Sample Adequacy: exploratory.

hasPart.name: Ebrahimkhani 2023: SCG for Aortic Valve Stenosis
hasPart.headline: Ebrahimkhani 2023: SCG for Aortic Valve Stenosis
hasPart.description: Demonstrates the capability of SCG to capture high-fidelity hemodynamic information previously thought to require advanced imaging.

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: validation. Sample Adequacy: exploratory.

hasPart.name: Parlato 2025: FOSTER Multimodal Dataset
hasPart.headline: Parlato 2025: FOSTER Multimodal Dataset
hasPart.description: S045 (FOSTER) is a high-quality 2025 dataset that represents the future of multimodal 'Force-based' monitoring. For OpenSCG, it provides the precise temporal alignment needed to train models that combine vibrations (SCG) with acoustic data (PCG), enabling more robust detection of heart valve events.

mentions.name: Total Data
mentions.value: 7 minutes per subject @ 10 kHz sampling rate

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: feasibility. Sample Adequacy: limited.

hasPart.name: Golenderov 2026: Rhythmic Spectrum Disorders in Field
hasPart.headline: Golenderov 2026: Rhythmic Spectrum Disorders in Field
hasPart.description: Demonstrates the effectiveness of advanced neural networks in processing heterogeneous and noisy smartphone sensor data.

mentions.name: Model
mentions.value: U-Net v3 with Multi-scale convolutions and Attention Gates

review.reviewBody: Evidence Role: validation. Sample Adequacy: strong.

hasPart.name: Agam 2022: Diastolic SCG vs Echo
hasPart.headline: Agam 2022: Diastolic SCG vs Echo
hasPart.description: Validates SCG’s potential for evaluating diastolic function. Note: This source was previously referenced as S038 in some contexts.

mentions.value: Only Ed amplitude significantly altered by tilt
